jokes.text: Boy, where are my Zydeco records? There's twelve more boxes, and they all sound the same!
jokes.setup: Homer looking for Zydeco records for Mardi Gras party
jokes.punchline: Admits all twelve boxes of Zydeco records sound identical
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Homer's obliviousness and the absurdity of having twelve identical-sounding records

jokes.text: What about my birthday party? This counts as that.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about his birthday party amid elaborate Mardi Gras preparations
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissively says the Mardi Gras party counts as Bart's birthday
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness delivered with perfect economy. Shows his priorities and casual dismissal of his son's special day

jokes.text: Super Jet Dinosaur Fun Monkeys
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about the difficulty of the float theme
jokes.punchline: The absurdly random combination of words that makes no coherent sense
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor typical of The Simpsons - random word combination that's funny because it's meaningless yet sounds like it should mean something

jokes.text: Just so you know, some of them act like they hate America. But what they really hate are Americans.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining his distant relatives he wants to bring to the party
jokes.punchline: The distinction between hating America vs. hating Americans
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation with perfect structure and timing. The distinction is both absurd and oddly insightful

jokes.text: It's my wife! Everyone, pretend this is a trade seminar in Zürich.
jokes.setup: Kirk at the party when his wife calls
jokes.punchline: Panic response with oddly specific lie about being at a Swiss trade seminar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kirk's pathetic marriage situation and tendency toward elaborate lies

jokes.text: Can't talk now, dear. It's four in the morning here in Zürich. Oh, right. We came together.
jokes.setup: Kirk trying to maintain the Zürich lie on the phone
jokes.punchline: Realizes the fatal flaw in his story - they came to the party together
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the previous joke showing Kirk's stupidity. The realization moment is beautifully timed

jokes.text: Here you go! Eat my food! Drink up my drink! Put your coat on my daughter's bed! I command you!
jokes.setup: Homer enthusiastically welcoming party guests
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal hospitality to bizarrely commanding people to put coats on Lisa's bed
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment showing his over-the-top enthusiasm and weird hosting priorities

jokes.text: As the worm said to the plate of spaghetti, 'I...'
jokes.setup: Ned starting what sounds like a folksy saying or joke
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off before delivering the punchline, leaving the setup hanging
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Ned's tendency toward folksy sayings, funny because we never get the payoff

jokes.text: Listen, we're having a party... I noticed. So anyway, if you're not doing anything, and it looks like you are, you're welcome to stop by. Or not.
jokes.setup: Homer awkwardly inviting Ned to the party
jokes.punchline: Backhanded invitation pointing out Ned looks like he's doing nothing, then immediately undercutting the invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer social awkwardness - manages to be insulting while trying to be nice

jokes.text: Man, you really are into me, huh? What am I? Some kind of talking dog?
jokes.setup: Ned agreeing to take a break from yard work to attend the party
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre response comparing himself to a talking dog
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Homer logic - completely random comparison that somehow fits his ego

jokes.text: It's Ash Wednesday, everybody! Sit down your gins, and confess your sins.
jokes.setup: Party transitioning from Mardi Gras to Ash Wednesday
jokes.punchline: Homer's rhyming command mixing drinking and religious observance
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay showing Homer's shallow understanding of religious traditions while maintaining party atmosphere

jokes.text: Because I have this magical thing called a home equity loan. I borrow all the money I want, and the house gets stuck with the bill. Sucker!
jokes.setup: Someone asking how Homer can afford the elaborate party every year
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misunderstanding of how home equity loans work, calling his own house a sucker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment combining his stupidity with timely satire about predatory lending. The 'Sucker!' directed at his own house is perfect

jokes.text: Fine, Mr. Skeptical. Give me back your beads. But, but, but... Beads, please.
jokes.setup: Ned questioning Homer's understanding of home equity loans
jokes.punchline: Homer's petty response demanding return of Mardi Gras beads, then Ned's pathetic resistance
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Homer's pettiness and Ned's politeness even when being mistreated

jokes.text: Marge, don't worry. It's like when we stopped paying the phone bill. They stopped calling us. In fact, everyone did.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to reassure Marge about their financial troubles
jokes.punchline: His 'solution' to phone bills resulted in complete social isolation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic showing how his 'solutions' create bigger problems. The escalation from bill collectors to everyone is perfect

jokes.text: That number's so big, it has a comma!
jokes.setup: Mortgage broker showing Homer his new payment amount
jokes.punchline: Homer's amazement at a four-digit number
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer financial illiteracy - finding a comma in a number remarkable shows his complete lack of money sense

jokes.text: Listen, you! When you gave me that money, you said I wouldn't have to repay it 'til the future! This isn't the future! It's the lousy stinking now!
jokes.setup: Homer confronting the mortgage broker about repayment
jokes.punchline: His bizarre temporal logic about when 'the future' actually arrives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic combining his stupidity with genuine frustration. The distinction between 'the future' and 'the lousy stinking now' is comedic gold

jokes.text: Hell, we fired our CEO, and he barely got out with fifty million dollars. That poor man! Is he okay? As okay as you can be in the north of France.
jokes.setup: Broker trying to show the bank's accountability by mentioning fired CEO
jokes.punchline: Homer's sincere concern for the CEO's 'hardship' of only getting $50 million
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire about corporate golden parachutes with Homer's misplaced sympathy providing the perfect comedic vehicle

jokes.text: Come on, Marge. You got the sun, you got hobo fire. I can't do all the work for you.
jokes.setup: Marge asking what to cook soup on
jokes.punchline: Broker's casual suggestion of 'hobo fire' as a cooking method while claiming he can't do all the work
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about homelessness delivered with perfect corporate callousness. 'Hobo fire' as a casual suggestion is brilliantly absurd

jokes.text: Hey, our old pair has to live somewhere. This crap shack makes your guest room look like winter palace.
jokes.setup: Patty and Selma at the foreclosure viewing
jokes.punchline: Comparing the Simpson house to their guest room, calling it a 'crap shack' but saying it's still an upgrade
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Patty and Selma's nastiness while also revealing their living situation is even worse

jokes.text: Don't worry, honey. We'll come out ahead. I ripped all the copper wire out of the walls. Did you remember to disconnect it first? Did I what now?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to salvage something from losing the house
jokes.punchline: Reveals he ripped out live electrical wires without disconnecting power first
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - his 'solution' to their problems involves extreme danger he didn't consider

jokes.text: The kid's height. Homer's weight.
jokes.setup: Family looking at growth chart marks on wall
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Homer's weight being marked at the same height as Lisa's growth
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual gag about Homer's weight being tracked alongside Lisa's height on the same wall chart

jokes.text: Anybody ever get murdered in this house? No. Thank you for your time.
jokes.setup: Potential buyer asking about the house's history
jokes.punchline: Immediately leaves upon hearing no murders occurred, as if that's a dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - the buyer wants a murder house, and the lack of murders is disappointing. Perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: I need a place to store my cufflinks.
jokes.setup: Auctioneer asking what bidder plans to do with the house
jokes.punchline: Wants to buy an entire house just to store cufflinks
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about extreme wealth - using an entire house for such a trivial purpose

jokes.text: Pass. I've already lifted this thing up once today. I'm not Hercules.
jokes.setup: Bidding continuing after someone outbids the rich man
jokes.punchline: Won't bid again because he already lifted his paddle once and claims he's not Hercules
jokes.explanation: Absurd extreme of laziness - comparing lifting an auction paddle to Herculean strength. Funny escalation of rich person problems

jokes.text: What is this, Randomly Tossing Your Paddle Day?
jokes.setup: People apparently throwing their paddles around chaotically
jokes.punchline: Auctioneer's exasperated reaction creating a fake holiday name for the chaos
jokes.explanation: Good professional frustration humor with the made-up holiday name being a nice touch

jokes.text: Ocean said to the dirt, 'I appreciate the sediment.'
jokes.setup: Ned making one of his folksy sayings
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'sentiment' vs 'sediment' in ocean/dirt context
jokes.explanation: Typical Ned corny pun delivered with his characteristic earnestness. Groan-worthy but fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Better let me go first. I left a little... surprise upstairs for the new owner.
jokes.setup: Family about to re-enter their house after Ned bought it
jokes.punchline: Homer left some kind of unpleasant surprise for the new owner (who turned out to be Ned)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's petty revenge that he now has to deal with himself. The euphemism 'surprise' makes it funnier

jokes.text: Evil triumphs again.
jokes.setup: Family celebrating Ned saving them
jokes.punchline: Baby Maggie's cynical commentary on the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Simpsons moment - baby making adult cynical observation with maximum economy. Classic show subversion

jokes.text: Hot comes out of hot! It's like I'm dreaming!
jokes.setup: Ned fixes their broken faucet
jokes.punchline: Marge's amazement that hot water comes from the hot tap
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how broken down their house was that basic plumbing functions are miraculous

jokes.text: Next problem.
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to fix leaky faucet with dramatic flourish
jokes.punchline: Visual reveals Homer's 'repair' made things dramatically worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence visual gag with perfect timing

jokes.text: It's irresistible! You ask him to do something and he does it. He's like a genie!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering Ned actually fulfills his landlord responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Treats basic competence and responsibility as magical genie powers
jokes.explanation: Good Homer perspective - his amazement at someone actually doing what they promise reveals his low expectations

jokes.text: Our house is up to code.
jokes.setup: Marge serving a celebratory cake
jokes.punchline: Cake inscription celebrating the basic achievement of meeting building codes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag about celebrating the bare minimum. Meeting code shouldn't be cake-worthy, making it perfectly absurd

jokes.text: You lounge around in your Egg McMansions, and we never see you unless the rent check's late.
jokes.setup: Homer angry at Ned for not coming over immediately for gas leak
jokes.punchline: Calls Ned's house an 'Egg McMansion' - combining McDonald's with McMansion
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau that's both insulting and absurd. The McDonald's reference makes it quintessentially Simpsons

jokes.text: You better just pray I don't find out where you live!
jokes.setup: Homer threatening Ned during their landlord dispute
jokes.punchline: Threatens to find where his next-door neighbor lives
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - threatening to find his neighbor's address while standing right next to his house. Classic angry Homer logic

jokes.text: If I was Marge's landlord, I'd fix up pipes, and I'd shower with flowers, and take her on a romantic getaway to the south seas, and I'd never return.
jokes.setup: Moe describing how he'd treat Marge if he was her landlord
jokes.punchline: Escalates from fixing pipes to romantic gestures to basically kidnapping her
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character moment showing his creepy obsession with Marge escalating from helpful to criminal

jokes.text: If I was Marge's landlord, I'd fix up pipes, and I'd shower with flowers, and take her on a romantic getaway to the south seas, and I'd never return.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining how he'd treat Marge as landlord
jokes.punchline: Escalates from repairs to increasingly inappropriate romantic fantasy
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe escalation with perfect character creepiness

jokes.text: We live in a house that is trying to kill us.
jokes.setup: Marge being interviewed for the news story about their house
jokes.punchline: Describes their house as actively malevolent and attempting murder
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of typical house problems into something more sinister. Marge's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: Who is this Dad you speak of, Lisa Simpson? You want a cookie, Dad? Yes.
jokes.setup: TV interview staging dramatic family dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Homer maintains denial while simultaneously responding to 'Dad'
jokes.explanation: Excellent contradiction showing Homer's simultaneous denial and hunger

jokes.text: I died when Homer sat on me. Why you little-- I'm the victim here.
jokes.setup: Haunted furniture giving testimony in news story
jokes.punchline: Couch claims to have died from Homer sitting on it, Homer gets defensive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment with talking furniture. Homer's defensive reaction to furniture complaining about his weight is perfect

jokes.text: Kadiddlehopper. It's not funny, that's how I swear!
jokes.setup: Ned angry at Homer, using his mild substitute swearing
jokes.punchline: Gets defensive about his made-up curse word when people find it amusing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - his innocent 'swearing' and defensive explanation about it not being funny is very him

jokes.text: We won't do it! Your leaving is our Christmas!
jokes.setup: Homer asking neighbors to buy the house for them
jokes.punchline: Neighbors are thrilled about the Simpsons leaving, comparing it to Christmas
jokes.explanation: Good neighborhood dynamics humor - the Simpsons are such bad neighbors that their departure is a holiday-level celebration

jokes.text: It is me, Jesus. I have come from my workshop in the North Pole to say, Don't evict the Simpsons.
jokes.setup: Bart disguised as Jesus trying to convince Ned not to evict them
jokes.punchline: Confuses Jesus with Santa Claus, saying Jesus has a workshop at the North Pole
jokes.explanation: Good Bart scheme with religious confusion. Mixing up Jesus and Santa shows his poor religious education despite living next to Ned

jokes.text: Finally, a practical use for my father.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering Grampa can prevent eviction by living with them
jokes.punchline: Homer's callous view of his father as a practical tool
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer humor about treating his elderly father as a legal loophole rather than family. Perfectly cynical

jokes.text: Whee. I'm gonna live like a human in a real house! What's the catch? I'm using you. For what? My own devices. All right.
jokes.setup: Grampa excited about living with family again
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission of exploitation met with Grampa's acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dysfunction with Grampa's resigned acceptance

jokes.text: Yeah, without the sun, my hair's reverting to its natural red! I don't wanna be called rusty again.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about being trapped indoors
jokes.punchline: Bart's hair color revelation and past nickname trauma
jokes.explanation: Good character detail with childhood trauma backstory

jokes.text: Family outing? I'll start. Lisa's gay!
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting a family outing
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstands 'outing' as revealing secrets rather than going out
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'outing' with Homer's characteristic misunderstanding. Lisa's sophisticated response makes it even better

jokes.text: Right. 'Cos I wouldn't wanna be gay like my heroes Billie Jean King, Susan Sontag, Sappho of Lesbos, and, I suspect, Peppermint Patty.
jokes.setup: Lisa responding to Homer calling her gay
jokes.punchline: Lists intellectual and cultural gay icons, ending with Peanuts character speculation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response showing her intelligence and cultural knowledge, with the Peppermint Patty reference being unexpectedly insightful

jokes.text: Now when I've got a downhill lie, I'd like to swing wide. Just like Patton did when he swung past Kesselring's boys in the Ardennes Forest.
jokes.setup: Eisenhower golf instruction video playing
jokes.punchline: Compares golf technique to WWII military strategy
jokes.explanation: Good historical character humor - Eisenhower naturally relating everything to his military experience, even golf

jokes.text: I know it seems that way because I'm such a vivid storyteller.
jokes.setup: Kids saying they know a story because they were there
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional belief in his storytelling abilities
jokes.explanation: Good Homer ego moment - his completely unwarranted confidence in abilities he clearly lacks

jokes.text: Because your father lives with me now. Flanders feeds me people food.
jokes.setup: Family discovering Grampa has moved in with Ned
jokes.punchline: Grampa's simple explanation prioritizing quality meals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa priorities with implication about Simpson family meals

jokes.text: The clothes they give us are the clothes we gave to charity last year.
jokes.setup: Family complaining about shelter conditions
jokes.punchline: Their donated clothes came back to them at the homeless shelter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic observation about the cycle of poverty and charity. Dark but insightful social commentary

jokes.text: Excellent credit, Christian, but not Baptist.
jokes.setup: Agent reviewing potential tenants' qualifications
jokes.punchline: Religious discrimination presented as a positive selling point
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire about housing discrimination and religious prejudice presented casually

jokes.text: Excellent credit, Christian, but not Baptist.
jokes.setup: Landlord reviewing Ned and new tenant's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Specific religious discrimination presented as positive qualification
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on religious prejudice in housing

jokes.text: Whacha got there, Woofy Goldberg?
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Ned with new potential tenants
jokes.punchline: Combines 'Whoopi Goldberg' with 'Woofy' for dog-related insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer name-mangling insult. Not particularly clever but fits his character and random cultural references

jokes.text: You can't be a saint unless you live among the lepers.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why he won't rent to nice tenants
jokes.punchline: Compares the Simpsons to lepers in his quest for sainthood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned religious philosophy - his martyrdom complex and viewing the Simpsons as his cross to bear

jokes.text: It's okay if you backslide a little. Never. Hurray for stupid Flanders!
jokes.setup: Homer promising to never abuse their friendship again
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with insulting cheer for Ned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment with instant promise breaking

jokes.text: I really hate this neighborhood.
jokes.setup: After witnessing the whole Simpsons/Flanders drama
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection of the neighborhood after seeing its dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective ending beat - outsider's perspective on the neighborhood's chaos. Good button to the episode
